name: Vegan Crab Noodle Soup

ingredients.item: block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu, mashed
vegetable or mushroom stock powder, plus more to taste
firm tofu, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes
fresh mushrooms (straw, beech, or king oyster), stems trimmed and sliced
vegetable or mushroom stock powder, plus more if needed
tamarind paste or 1 teaspoon tamarind concentrate mixed with 2 teaspoons water
leaf or iceberg lettuce, leaves torn into small pieces or shredded, for serving
lime, cut into wedges, for serving

method: RIÊU In a medium bowl, combine mashed tofu, 1 tablespoon of sugar, 1 teaspoon of stock powder, and 2 teaspoons of the annatto oil, and mix well.

Heat the remaining 2 teaspoons annatto oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat. When hot, add tofu mixture, stir, and cook until tofu absorbs all seasoning (about 3 minutes). Transfer to a bowl and set aside.

Heat ½ cup (120 ml) of the vegetable oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium-low heat. Add tofu cubes and cook until golden brown on all sides (10 to 15 minutes). Transfer to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.

In a medium stockpot, heat remaining ½ cup (120 ml) vegetable oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until fragrant (about 1 minute). Add tomatoes and mushrooms, stir, and cook until soft (about 3 minutes). Add vegetable broth, salt, and stock powder.
Add tamarind paste, deep-fried tofu, and 1 tablespoon of sugar and bring to a boil. Cook over medium-low heat for 5 minutes. Season to taste with more salt and/or stock powder if needed.
If using, place chao and fermented soybean sauce in a food processor or blender and process with the water until smooth. (You can also mash by hand with a spoon.) Stir mixture into broth.
Follow the instructions on the package to cook the dried rice vermicelli noodles.

Divide the cooked noodles into serving bowls, spoon the riêu on top, and then ladle the hot broth over the top. Serve with a platter of shredded lettuce and perilla leaves with lime wedges on the side.
